perturbation.original_text: The Company agrees that for a period of twelve (12) months following the date of this Agreement, it will not solicit and will not permit any of its Affiliates (in each case who is or becomes aware of the possible Transaction or is otherwise provided with Confidential Information) to solicit for employment any Specified Employee (as defined below); provided , however , that this Section 10 will not prevent the Company from (a) causing to be placed any general advertisement or similar notice that is not targeted specifically at employees of Meru or its subsidiaries, (b) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit persons for employment on their behalf and soliciting the employment of any Specified Employee who is identified by such recruiting firm or organization, so long as such recruiting firm or organization is not instructed to target or solicit any Specified Employee of Meru or its subsidiaries, or (c) engaging in employment discussions or hiring a Specified Employee if such Specified Employee initiates contact with the Company by clear and convincing evidence.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company agrees that for a period of six (6) months following the date of this Agreement, it will not solicit and will not permit any of its Affiliates (in each case who is or becomes aware of the possible Transaction or is otherwise provided with Confidential Information) to solicit for employment any Specified Employee (as defined below); provided , however , that this Section 10 will not prevent the Company from (a) causing to be placed any general advertisement or similar notice that is not targeted specifically at employees of Meru or its subsidiaries, (b) engaging any recruiting firm or similar organization to identify or solicit persons for employment on their behalf and soliciting the employment of any Specified Employee who is identified by such recruiting firm or organization, so long as such recruiting firm or organization is not instructed to target or solicit any Specified Employee of Meru or its subsidiaries, or (c) engaging in employment discussions or hiring a Specified Employee if such Specified Employee initiates contact with the Company by clear and convincing evidence.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the non-solicitation period from twelve months to six may not provide adequate protection of trade secrets, especially if key employees possess crucial confidential information.
perturbation.law_explanation: While a six-month non-solicitation agreement is not automatically illegal, it may be deemed unreasonable and unenforceable under the Defend Trade Secrets Act, particularly if it fails to adequately protect legitimately held trade secrets. If Meru can prove that the shorter duration exposes critical confidential information to a high risk of misappropriation, it could challenge the enforceability of this clause. The reasonableness depends on factors such as the sensitivity of the information, the employee's role, and the competitive landscape.

Civil proceedings (a) The Attorney General may, in a civil action, obtain appropriate injunctive relief against any violation of this chapter. (b) Private Civil Actions.- (1) In general .-An owner of a trade secret that is misappropriated may bring a civil action under this subsection if the trade secret is related to a product or service used in, or intended for use in, interstate or foreign commerce. (2) Civil seizure.- (A) In
perturbation.justification: The original contract text provided a twelve-month non-solicitation period, which was reduced to six months in the changed text. According to the perturbation explanation, this reduction might not adequately protect trade secrets. The law explanation suggests this could violate the Defend Trade Secrets Act if it exposes critical confidential information. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 18 USC 1836, allows the Attorney General to obtain injunctive relief against violations related to trade secrets. The modified contract presents an inconsistency that weakens the protection of trade secrets, potentially creating a legal contradiction because the six-month period may be deemed unreasonable and unenforceable, thus undermining the intent of the law to protect trade secrets.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within that state, and without regard to conflicts of laws principles of that state. Each of the parties hereby agrees and irrevocably consents to personal jurisdiction and venue in any federal or state court within Wilmington, Delaware, having subject matter jurisdiction, for the purposes of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement. Each party further waives and agrees not to plead that any such action in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum. To the fullest extent permitted by law, each of the parties hereby agrees to waive trial by jury in any action, proceeding or counterclaim brought by or on behalf of either party with respect to any matter whatsoever relating to this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall be governed by and construed and enforced in accordance with the laws of the State of California applicable to agreements made and to be performed entirely within that state, and without regard to conflicts of laws principles of that state. Each of the parties hereby agrees and irrevocably consents to personal jurisdiction and venue in any federal or state court within Wilmington, Delaware, having subject matter jurisdiction, for the purposes of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this agreement. Each party further waives and agrees not to plead that any such action in any such court has been brought in an inconvenient forum. To the fullest extent permitted by law, each of the parties hereby agrees to waive trial by jury in any action, proceeding or counterclaim brought by or on behalf of either party with respect to any matter whatsoever relating to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to California while retaining Delaware as the venue and jurisdiction can create a conflict of laws issue.
perturbation.law_explanation: While not directly violating the statute, specifying California law while mandating Delaware venue and jurisdiction may lead to a motion to stay or dismiss the action under California Code of Civil Procedure § 410.30, arguing that Delaware is an inconvenient forum given that California law applies. This contradiction between the chosen law and forum can introduce legal challenges.

OF CIVIL ACTIONS [307 - 1062.34] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 5. JURISDICTION AND SERVICE OF PROCESS [410.10 - 418.11] ( Title 5 added by Stats. 1969, Ch. 1610. ) CHAPTER 1. Jurisdiction and Forum [410.10 - 410.70] ( Heading of Chapter 1 amended by Stats. 1992, Ch. 615, Sec. 3. ) ARTICLE 2. Forum [410.30 - 410.42] ( Heading of Article 2 amended by Stats. 1992, Ch. 615, Sec. 4. ) 410.30. (a) When a court upon motion of a party or its own motion finds that in the interest of substantial justice an action should be heard in a forum outside this state, the court shall stay or dismiss the action in whole or in part on any conditions that may be just. (b) The provisions of Section 418.10 do not apply to a motion to stay or dismiss the action by a defendant who has made a general appearance. (Amended by Stats. 1972, Ch. 601. Note: This version was suspended from Sept. 22, 1986, until Jan. 1, 1992, during operation of the temporary amendment by Stats. 1986, Ch. 968.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies California law while retaining Delaware as the venue. The Law Explanation suggests this could lead to a motion to stay or dismiss under California Code of Civil Procedure § 410.30 due to Delaware being an inconvenient forum given California law applies. Scraped Law Snippet 1, CCP 410.30(a), states a court may stay or dismiss an action if it finds that in the interest of substantial justice, the action should be heard in a forum outside the state. This supports the argument that the contradictory choice of law and venue introduces legal challenges and could lead to the action being dismissed or stayed, thus creating a legal contradiction.
